notes: Recording police in Germany is allowed in some situations, but sharing or distributing footage without consent can be restricted.
Home CCTV is allowed in Germany in limited situations, but strict privacy laws apply, especially if cameras capture public spaces or neighbouring property.
Employee monitoring is allowed in Germany only under strict conditions, with strong protections for worker privacy.
Recording private conversations without consent is illegal in Germany, even if you are part of the conversation.
Recording video in public is restricted in Germany, especially if individuals can be identified, due to strong privacy protections.
Recording phone calls without consent from all parties is illegal in Germany.
GPS tracking another person without their consent is generally illegal in Germany due to strong privacy protections.
Drone use is legal in Germany under strict aviation and privacy rules, but using drones for surveillance can be heavily restricted.
Business CCTV is allowed in Germany only under strict conditions, including necessity, transparency, and compliance with data protection laws.
Hidden cameras are generally illegal in Germany, especially when used to secretly record people without their knowledge or consent.
